This is the Gale Encyclopedia of Medicine, Second Edition — a comprehensive five-volume medical reference work spanning over 3,600 pages. Medical encyclopedias don't have arguments — they catalog information alphabetically.

This is a reference work, not a persuasive text. The Gale Encyclopedia of Medicine organizes medical knowledge from A-Z across five volumes to make specialized information accessible to general readers, treating all medical topics as equally worthy of systematic documentation.

The encyclopedia's core principle is **democratic access to medical knowledge** — it assumes any medical concept, from common diseases
